_meta.subject: 8 Jungian cognitive functions
_meta.verified: adversarially fact-checked by a second agent against primary sources (deepinme research workflow, 2026-07)
_meta.source: see reference/sources.md and _meta of related item files

functions.name: Extraverted Sensing
functions.definition: Engages fully with immediate, concrete sensory experience of the physical environment; notices what is real and present and responds to it in the moment.

functions.name: Introverted Sensing
functions.definition: Compares present experience against stored past impressions and detailed internal records; values memory, consistency, routine, and tradition.

functions.name: Extraverted Intuition
functions.definition: Scans the outer world for patterns, possibilities, and novel connections; generates ideas, alternatives, and what-if scenarios.

functions.name: Introverted Intuition
functions.definition: Synthesizes information into unconscious insights, underlying meanings, and long-range visions; converges on a single future implication or interpretation.

functions.name: Extraverted Thinking
functions.definition: Organizes the external world with objective logic, structure, and efficiency; makes decisions by measurable results, plans, and standards.

functions.name: Introverted Thinking
functions.definition: Analyzes internally for logical precision and consistency; builds and refines a personal framework of principles to understand how things truly work.

functions.name: Extraverted Feeling
functions.definition: Attends to others' emotions and to social harmony; makes decisions based on shared values, group needs, and maintaining connection.

functions.name: Introverted Feeling
functions.definition: Evaluates by deeply held personal values and authenticity; focuses on inner emotional integrity and staying true to what feels right.
